danger_signals.visual_form: player sprite position relative to puck carrier
danger_signals.meaning: immediate threat to possession or physical check

danger_signals.visual_form: goalie sprite blocks net opening visually
danger_signals.meaning: shot difficulty increases based on visible coverage
player sent to penalty box; team disadvantaged numerically

safety_signals.visual_form: unoccupied sprite space on playing field
safety_signals.meaning: opportunity for pass or skating without immediate opposition

safety_signals.visual_form: allied player sprite in advantageous formation
safety_signals.meaning: receiving option for pass or support play

safety_signals.visual_form: player sprites clustered in defensive end
safety_signals.meaning: team defending home territory; lower immediate offensive threat

faction_indicators.color_identity: primary jersey color (varies by team roster)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: scoreboard displays team abbreviation and score; bench positioning on designated side
opposite bench; displayed in away slot on scoreboard UI

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: puck sprite animates across ice; player sprites move freely in response
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: game clock counts down; players engage in dynamic positioning and collision detection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: puck sprite centers on ice; player sprites freeze in formal stance around center circle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: input locked until faceoff initiated; visual tension of opposing centers poised

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: penalty indicator on UI; offending player sprite repositioned to penalty box area off-ice
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: penalized player unavailable for play; team operates with numerical disadvantage (4v5 or 3v4)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: puck animation enters goal aperture; scoreboard updates visually; potential celebration sprite animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: play halts; delayed reset to center-ice faceoff; score reflected in UI

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: final score displayed prominently; game clock frozen at 0:00
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: input disabled; transition to result screen or menu state

environmental_storytelling.example: real-time display of period, time remaining, and team scores communicates match progression and winning/losing state
jersey numbers and sprite animations indicate which players are active; substitutions communicate strategic lineup changes
puck sprite behavior and skate animation smoothness convey play momentum and ice surface state (implicit)
puck proximity to sprite indicates control; visual trailing or loose puck animation shows contested possession

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: bounces/slides erratically; neutral sprite movement without directional input; signals scramble for possession
responds immediately to input; sprite orientation matches intended direction; animation loops for skating/stopping
sprite moves toward puck carrier; performs body checking animation; closes distance rapidly
sprite maintains position in front of net; reduces forward movement; prioritizes blocking passing lanes visually
sprite faces play; animation frames show stance variation based on shot proximity; position shifts to block visible openings
sprite remains stationary in penalty box area; removed from animated play loop; grayed or distinct visual presentation (if implemented)

notes: NHL Hockey (Genesis, 1992) uses minimalist 16-bit sprite-based visual communication. World-state and danger/safety signals rely on spatial positioning, jersey color contrast, and UI elements rather than complex animation or particle effects. The paradigm is competitive team sports; faction identity is strictly team-based with no narrative political dimension. Environmental storytelling is procedural through match progression rather than narrative world-building. The game communicates state through functional visual hierarchy: scoreboard > active play area > player sprites > puck sprite.
